replication_study.hypothesis: Participants reminded of their own mortality (death-thought condition) will produce a lower proportion of utilitarian responses than control participants (pain-thought condition) for moral-conflict dilemmas but not for morally neutral control dilemmas; operationalised as a significant Salience (death vs pain) × Scenario Type (conflict vs control) interaction on the binary judgement ‘utilitarian acceptable (1) / not acceptable (0)’.

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single cross-section, no waves; we use the full cleaned sample that passed the manipulation-check filter contained in the CSV.
data_plan.sample_size: N = 131 raw; expected analytic N ≈ 115 after manipulation-check filter (see Rmd melt → filter(satisfactory_manipulation_response1 == 1)).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Scenario-level judgement nested in participants (2 judgements per participant).
data_plan.access_details: CSV file bundled in /app/data/original/35/input/replication_data; freely available within this project.

qualification.explanation: The dataset recreates the original mortality-salience manipulation and utilitarian-judgement task with independent participants, providing a new sample for robustness testing while keeping procedures comparable.
qualification.similarity_to_original:
• Same manipulation: ‘death’ vs ‘pain’ writing tasks (variables death_salience, pain_salience; see CSV columns) correspond to original mortality-salience vs control manipulation (post_registration.json → independent_variables). 
• Same outcome: proportion/acceptability of utilitarian action on two versions of each dilemma (moral_accept, moral_accept1) aligned with original harm-conflict and harm-control scenarios (initial_details.txt). 
• Same experimental factorial structure enabling Salience×Scenario ANOVA (Rmd code chunk ‘anova_test’, matching original F(1,83)=6.89 interaction).
qualification.deviation_from_original:
• Recruitment: online Qualtrics panel (CSV metadata StartDate, IPAddress) versus in-person campus lab sample in Toulouse (post_registration.json → data/source).
• Language/cultural setting likely English-speaking online respondents vs French students; increases external validity.
• Slightly larger raw N = 131 vs original Experiment 1 N = 85.
• Replication file includes gender, politics, etc., allowing additional controls not present originally.

data_plan.notes: Variables already in wide form for each dilemma then reshaped to long in Rmd; salience recorded redundantly as cond (1/2) and salience (text). Binary judgements stored as strings ('1','2'), converted to 0/1 by R code. No obvious missing-value coding issues beyond manipulation-check removal.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load CSV into R.
2. Reshape to long format with one row per participant-scenario (follow Rmd melt).
3. Exclude participants failing the manipulation-check (satisfactory_manipulation_response1 ≠ 1).
4. Recode moral_acceptability to binary 0/1 (1 = not moral, 2 = moral → 0/1 variable).
5. Fit mixed-effects logistic regression with random intercept for participant: moral_acceptability_01 ~ salience*moral_scenario + (1|participant_uid).
6. Extract interaction term coefficient and Wald χ² / z test.
7. For direct comparability with original, additionally compute 2×2 mixed ANOVA on participant-level percentages using rstatix::anova_test (as in Rmd) and record F, p, η²p.
8. Compare directionality, significance, and effect-size magnitude to original F(1,83)=6.89, p=.01, η²p=.08.
planned_method.models: Primary: mixed-effects logistic regression (glmer, family=binomial). Secondary: 2×2 mixed ANOVA on binary outcome averaged per cell.
planned_method.independent_variables: salience (death vs pain), moral_scenario (conflict vs control).
planned_method.control_variables: Random intercept for participant_uid; optional demographic covariates (age, gender) if imbalance detected.
planned_method.tools_software: R (≥4.1) with tidyverse, lme4, lmerTest, sjPlot, rstatix.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Interaction coefficient (log-odds) and odds ratio; ANOVA interaction F and η²p.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z (logistic) and F-test (ANOVA); α = 0.05, two-tailed.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion after manipulation-check filter and removal of any NA in outcome.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not required – single focal interaction; any exploratory follow-ups will use Holm correction.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replication is successful if Salience×Scenario interaction is negative (death reduces utilitarian responses in conflict but not control) and p < 0.05 in either logistic or ANOVA approaches; effect direction must match original.

files.Tremoliere_generalizability_score.Rmd: End-to-end RMarkdown performing data cleaning, descriptive graphs, mixed-effects logistic model (fit10), and ANOVA replication (fit4). Reads the CSV in the same folder.
files.analysis_script.R (to be written): Thin wrapper that sources the critical code chunks from the Rmd or executes equivalent R code non-interactively, enabling automated container execution.

codebase.notes: All paths must reference '/app/data/original/35/input/replication_data'. The Rmd already does so implicitly by reading the CSV in the working directory; Docker image will set workdir to that folder.

analysis.instructions:
1. docker run --rm -v $(pwd)/data:/app/data -w /app/data/original/35/input/replication_data rocker/tidyverse:4.3.1 Rscript analysis_script.R
2. Script reads CSV, performs cleaning, fits models, and prints interaction statistics.
3. Save results table to /app/data/tremoliere_replication_results.csv for comparison.
analysis.comparison_metrics:
• Direction of interaction coefficient (should be negative).
• p-value (<0.05).
• Effect size: odds ratio <1 and/or η²p similar magnitude (~0.08).
